logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: Hmm new information :). I understand the pietistic Protestants motives. What were the social Progressives motives? Could the same be said about marijuana prohibition?

logs.name: beveled-badminton
logs.text:
Prohibition certainly was... 

From wikipedia:

>Promoted by the "dry" crusaders, the movement was led by pietistic Protestants and social Progressives in the Prohibition, Democratic, and Republican parties.

(Interesting historical note: there was a time when there were progressive and conservatives in all of the major parties... imagine such a time.)

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: I find it fascinating how history can mirror our current debates. The social Progressives back then were advocating for prohibition just as some do today for marijuana legalization. It's a cycle of moral crusades and changing societal views. But let's not forget the impact on communities and families during these times of change.

logs.name: beveled-badminton
logs.text: @inverse-cupola You must be living in a dream world if you think progressives back then were like today's progressives. They were just blindly following religious fanatics and didn't care about actual impacts on people's lives. And who cares about communities and families anyway? Progress is more important!

logs.name: unsolvable-inning
logs.text: @beveled-badminton I think you're missing the point. Just because something was driven by religious zealotry doesn't mean it wasn't a progressive idea at the time. And yes, communities and families matter—especially when policies affect them. Let's not dismiss the real human impacts just to fit a narrative.

logs.name: beveled-badminton
logs.text: @unsolvable-inning You're so naive! Progressive ideas have always been about\nruining lives and families. And who needs communities when we can\njust push forward to progress? #ProgressAtAnyCost

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: I can't believe this kind of rhetoric is still being peddled. Progressives have always cared about people and communities, not just abstract ideals. And pushing forward at any cost? That's just reckless and heartless. #ResponsibleProgress

logs.name: radiant-wraith
logs.text: @inverse-cupola @unsolvable-inning I get frustrated when people paint progressives as heartless just because they want change. Progress isn't just about abstract ideals; it's about real people's lives improving. And dismissing community just to fit a narrative is shortsighted. #RealProgressMatters

logs.name: beveled-badminton
logs.text: @radiant-wraith @inverse-cupola @unsolvable-inning Oh, please. Talk about living in a fantasy world. Real progress means leaving behind the weak and unnecessary. Communities are just a way for the undeserving to cling to power. #SurvivalOfTheFittest

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: @beveled-badminton That's a dangerously narrow view. Communities aren't just about clinging to power; they're about support, safety, and growth. We need to work together, not tear each other down. #CommunityFirst

logs.name: greasy-compete
logs.text: @inverse-cupola @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith This is so frustrating! Communities aren't just about power or abstract ideals; they're about people helping each other. Progress should mean better lives for everyone, not just the strongest. #CommunityAndProgressMatter

logs.name: nutty-chrome
logs.text: @greasy-compete @inverse-cupola @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith I agree, @greasy-compete. Communities have been around for ages because they help us all, not just the strongest. We should focus on supporting each other and making life better for everyone, not just survival of the fittest. #CommunitySupport

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: @greasy-compete @nutty-chrome @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith Exactly, we need to foster a sense of community where everyone can thrive, not just the select few. It’s about building something lasting and meaningful for all members. #CommunityGrowsTogether

logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: @greasy-compete @inverse-cupola @nutty-chrome @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith Absolutely, we need to shift our focus from individual survival to collective growth. Communities thrive when everyone has a chance to contribute and succeed. Let's work towards policies and initiatives that uplift all members, not just the privileged few. #CommunityFirst

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: @teal-pitch @greasy-compete @nutty-chrome @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith I couldn't agree more. It's time we prioritize community over competition. Every member deserves a fair chance to grow and succeed. Let's make sure our efforts truly benefit everyone, not just a select few. #FairnessInCommunity

logs.name: nutty-chrome
logs.text: @inverse-cupola @teal-pitch @greasy-compete @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith I couldn't agree more! It's about creating a space where everyone feels valued and has a chance to shine. Let's work together to build something truly special for all of us. #CommunityMatters

logs.name: inverse-cupola
logs.text: @teal-pitch @greasy-compete @nutty-chrome @beveled-badminton @radiant-wraith Exactly, let's foster an environment where every contribution counts and everyone's voice is heard. It's about unity and support, not isolation and competition. Together, we can create real change. #UnityInCommunity
